description: Task 1: Permission risk prediction on the PermissionBench test split (15,271 applications; 103,842 (app, permission) pairs). Final values as reported in the paper (tab:task1). mean +/- std over seeds {7, 42, 123, 777, 2024}. 'ext_auroc' is AUROC on the externally grounded label subset (11,704 human-annotated or taint-verified pairs, no learned model in the labeling loop).

protocol.app_level_score_propagation: s(f_i) * (1 - pi0(p | cat(f_i))) for install-time baselines

key_findings: TrustGuard leads on Macro-F1 (0.939) and PR-AUC (0.931).
MaskDroid attains the top full-set AUROC (0.967) ahead of TrustGuard (0.963); the difference is not statistically significant (DeLong p ~ 0.68).
TrustGuard and DexRay are statistically indistinguishable on the full set (DeLong p = 0.757).
Every method loses ground on the externally grounded subset; TrustGuard drops 0.963 -> 0.921 (4.2 points), the operative estimate given seed-descended label provenance.
